answer:
Well, when it comes to music, I've always been drawn to indie pop. It's just so vibrant and filled with emotion, you know? Artists like Phoebe Bridgers and Tame Impala really resonate with me. Their sound has this perfect blend of catchy melodies and thoughtful lyrics that you can really connect with on a personal level. I mean, I find their music not only enjoyable but also quite introspective, which feels really important, especially during the transition from high school to university.

Indie pop, in particular, has this unique way of capturing everyday experiences and emotions. You can relate to the highs and lows of life through their songs. For instance, I remember listening to Phoebe Bridgers' "Motion Sickness" during a tough time; it just articulated everything I was feeling. This genre helps people express themselves and feel understood, which is so crucial, I think. 

Plus, the community around indie music is fantastic. Going to concerts or local gigs, you can really feel that sense of belonging. Everyone's there enjoying the same vibe, and it creates these amazing shared experiences. So, yeah, I believe music like this not only entertains but also helps many of us navigate our emotions and connect with others. Ultimately, it's about discovering who you are through the songs that speak to you, and for me, indie pop does exactly that.
title: What kind of music would you like to play? Who are your favorite musicians in this style? How does this music help people?
